text.title: Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) Generation and Maintenance Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the requirements-traceability methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (lock vocabulary): Define role vocabulary + version; freeze.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the lock vocabulary gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (seed edges): Add edges per role; persist as YAML.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the seed edges gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (forward and backward pass): Compute coverage + orphans both directions.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the forward and backward pass gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (publish rtm): Render RTM markdown + coverage report.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the publish rtm gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (maintain on change): RTM update step gated in change-control.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the maintain on change gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for requirements-traceability do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
